slides.description: The slide is titled 'AI Screenplays' in large, bold blue text, centered at the top. Below the title is a large, colorful illustration depicting a scene from the South Park cartoon, specifically an office. In the foreground, a gray-haired man in a suit is standing next to a cardboard robot labeled 'A.W.E.S.O.M.-O'. The office is lavishly decorated with a large wooden desk on the left holding Oscar and Emmy-like trophies, along with a golden Cartman trophy. Through a large window in the background, the 'HOLLYWOOD' sign is visible on a distant mountain range at sunset. To the right of the man, there's a globe, a fish tank with an alien inside, and a safe. On the wall, a framed picture displays the text 'AWESOME-O LAME'. On the desk in front of the man, there's a brown notebook, a partially visible script, a vintage telephone, and a framed photo featuring several South Park characters.

The slide is titled 'Generating Long Documents' in large, bold blue text, centered at the top. Below the title, there are four black, left-aligned bullet points. The first bullet states: 'AI can be used to generate long, coherent text documents'. The second bullet provides examples: 'Books, reports, screenplays'. The third bullet outlines the presentation's focus: 'Today we will build an agentic flow to generate screenplays for movies'. The fourth and final bullet indicates broader applicability: 'This flow architecture can be generalized to other long text documents'.

The slide is titled 'Challenge: "One-Shot" Long-Form' in large, bold blue text, centered at the top. Below the title, there are two main black, left-aligned bullet points. The first bullet states: 'It is difficult for an AI to generate a long coherent document in one-shot'. The second bullet reads: 'Local vs global – the AI is good at writing small parts, but cannot maintain coherence across whole document'. This second bullet has two indented sub-bullets: '– Becomes “lost in the middle”' and '– Context drift – deep into the document, the AI can hallucinate or lose focus of what to write'.

The slide is titled 'Movie Screenplays' in large, bold blue text, centered at the top. Below the title, there are two main black, left-aligned bullet points. The first main bullet states: 'Screenplays have many different elements the AI needs to capture'. This bullet has three indented sub-bullets: '– Story shape', '– Goals of “sequences” in the movie', and '– Scene action and dialogue'. The second main bullet states: 'Hierarchical structure of a screenplay'. This bullet has four indented sub-bullets: '– Premise – basic elements of the movie', '– Story arc – high level plot structure', '– Sequences – more detailed plot', and '– Scenes – even more detailed plot'.

The slide is titled 'Screenplay Flow' in large, bold blue text, centered at the top. Below the title, a horizontal flowchart diagram illustrates the stages of a screenplay generation process. The flow begins on the left with a blue rectangular box labeled 'Premise', displaying 'premise.json' below the label and a small robot icon. An arrow points from 'Premise' to a purple rectangular box labeled 'Arc', which displays 'arc.json' and a robot icon. From 'Arc', two arrows emerge: one points horizontally to an orange rectangular box labeled 'Sequences', displaying 'sequence.json' and a robot icon; the other arrow points downwards to a green rectangular box labeled 'Scenes', displaying 'scenes.json' and a robot icon.

The slide is titled 'Premise Agent' in large, bold blue text, centered at the top. In the top right corner, there is a small image of the A.W.E.S.O.M.-O cardboard robot from South Park. Below the title, there are four black, left-aligned bullet points. The first bullet states: 'A chat agent that discusses with you what your movie is about'. This bullet has an indented sub-bullet: '– Title, logline (like an executive summary), characters, genre, central conflict'. The second bullet reads: 'You can ask the agent to give ideas for the plot if you are stuck'. The third bullet states: 'This chat can take a few minutes even if you have a general sense about your movie (takes time to specify all the major elements of the movie)'. The fourth and final bullet reads: 'This agent then creates a file premise.json'.

The slide is titled 'Premise.json' in large, bold blue text, centered at the top. Below the title, a code snippet is displayed against a dark background, formatted like a JSON file in a code editor. The JSON content is: 'project_id': 'shadow_of_the_dark_world_20260402_025456', 'title': 'Shadow of the Dark World', 'writers': ['TBD'], 'source_script': null, 'format_notes': 'Awesome-O generated this from a CLI chat.', 'logline': 'A seemingly ordinary young man, Link, is thrust into a perilous quest after his uncle is gravely wounded rescuing Princess Zelda. He must gather legendary artifacts, wield the fabled Master Sword, and confront a malevolent wizard to prevent the dark lord Ganon’s release and the conquest of their world.', 'genre': ['fantasy', 'action', 'adventure'], 'themes': ['hero’s journey', 'destiny', 'courage in the face of overwhelming evil', 'good versus evil', 'sacrifice'].

The slide is titled 'Premise.json' in large, bold blue text, centered at the top. Below the title, a code snippet is displayed against a dark background, formatted like a JSON file in a code editor. This is a continuation of the JSON content from the previous slide. The visible JSON content includes: 'central_conflict': 'Link's race against time to understand and embrace his role as a hero, stopping the sorcerer Aghanim from sacrificing Princess Zelda and six other maidens, a ritual that would unleash the ancient evil Ganon from the corrupted Dark World upon the Light World.', 'setting' which contains 'primary_time': 'High fantasy era, contemporary-feel for characters' and 'primary_places' as an array: ['Hyrule Castle', 'Kakariko Village', 'Eastern Palace', 'Lost Woods', 'Dark World']. It also shows 'protagonists' as an array containing an object with 'name': 'Link', 'role': 'Unassuming hero, destined to wield the Master Sword', and 'want': 'To save his uncle, rescue Zelda, and ultimately prevent Aghanim and Ganon from destroying Hyrule.' The JSON snippet is truncated at the bottom, indicating more content beyond the visible area.

The slide is titled 'Premise.json' in large, bold blue text, centered at the top. Below the title, a code snippet is displayed against a dark background, formatted like a JSON file in a code editor. This is a continuation of the JSON content from the previous slides. The visible JSON content includes the 'protagonists' array, showing an object for 'Link' with his 'name', 'role', and 'want'. It then lists 'key_antagonist' as an object for 'Aghanim', detailing his 'name', 'role' as 'Evil sorcerer, Ganon's pawn', and 'want' to 'seal the seven maidens, including Princess Zelda, to open a portal to the Dark World and release Ganon.' Next, 'key_ally' is shown as an object for 'Sahashrahla' (Sahasrahla), describing his 'name', 'role' as 'Village elder, wise man, guide', and 'want' to 'guide Link to acquire the Master Sword and stop Aghanim's plans to release Ganon.' Finally, the 'stakes' are defined as 'The total conquest of the Light World by the dark lord Ganon, resulting in the enslavement or destruction of its inhabitants.' The JSON snippet is truncated at the bottom, indicating more content beyond the visible area.

The slide is titled 'Arc Agent' in large, bold blue text, centered at the top. In the top right corner, there is a small image of the A.W.E.S.O.M.-O cardboard robot from South Park. Below the title, there are four black, left-aligned bullet points. The first bullet states: 'A chat agent that discusses your movie story arc'. This bullet has two indented sub-bullets: '– Arc consists of multiple acts' and '– Agent takes premise.json as input for context'. The second bullet reads: 'You tell the agent what happens in each act, then ask it to draft the act, one at a time'. The third bullet states: 'Premise file gives it context for the story arc'. The fourth and final bullet reads: 'This agent then creates a file arc.json'.

The slide is titled 'Arc.json' in large, bold blue text, centered at the top. Below the title, a code snippet is displayed against a dark background, formatted like a JSON file in a code editor. This is a continuation of the JSON content for the 'Arc' file. The visible JSON content shows an object representing 'act': 3. Inside this object, there are key-value pairs including 'label': 'Battle Aghanim and the Dark World', 'approx_pages_note': 'This act, encompassing the climax and a major cliffhanger, will likely be around 20-30 pages of a 120-page screenplay.', 'dramatic_question': 'Can Link defeat Aghanim, save Zelda and the other maidens, and prevent Ganon from conquering their world, even when cast into the heart of darkness?', and a detailed 'summary' of the act. It also includes 'turning_point_end': 'Aghanim banishes Link to the corrupted Dark World, setting the stage for a new, desperate quest as the ultimate evil, Ganon, looms large.', and a list of 'must_hit_beats'. The JSON snippet ends with 'midpoint_note': null, and is not truncated at the bottom.

The slide is titled 'Sequence Agent' in large, bold blue text, centered at the top. In the top right corner, there is a small image of the A.W.E.S.O.M.-O cardboard robot from South Park. Below the title, there are four black, left-aligned bullet points. The first bullet states: 'An automated agent (no chat) that writes the sequences of the movie for each act'. The second bullet reads: 'Inputs per sequence'. This bullet has two indented sub-bullets: '– Premise.json, arc.json – give agent global view of the movie' and '– All previous sequences generated so far – give agent local context for what should happen next'. The third bullet states: 'Primary sequence elements'. This bullet has three indented sub-bullets: '– Goal – what is the narrative purpose of this sequence', '– Key locations – locations in the sequence', and '– Exit beat – how the sequence ends and transitions to next sequence'. The fourth and final bullet reads: 'This agent then creates a file sequence.json'.

The slide is titled 'Sequence.json' in large, bold blue text, centered at the top. Below the title, a code snippet is displayed against a dark background, formatted like a JSON file in a code editor. The visible JSON content begins with 'project_id': 'shadow_of_the_dark_world_20260402_025456', 'sequence_count': 8, and 'note': null. It then shows the start of a 'sequences' array, containing an object for the first sequence. This object has 'id': 'SEQ1', 'act': 1, 'title': 'Zelda's Urgent Plea', and a 'goal' described as 'To establish Link’s call to adventure, his initial motivation (saving his uncle and Zelda), and to equip him with his first tools for the perilous journey ahead.' It also includes 'emotional_tone': 'Urgent, sorrowful, determined', 'key_locations' as an array listing 'Link’s Home' and 'Hyrule Castle (Outer Walls)', and an 'exit_beat' stating: 'Armed with his uncle’s sword and shield, Link steels himself, ready to infiltrate the formidable Hyrule Castle and rescue Princess Zelda.'

The slide is titled 'Scene Agent' in large, bold blue text, centered at the top. In the top right corner, there is a small image of the A.W.E.S.O.M.-O cardboard robot from South Park. Below the title, there are four black, left-aligned bullet points. The first bullet states: 'An automated agent (no chat) that writes the scenes of the movie for each sequence'. The second bullet reads: 'Inputs per sequence'. This bullet has two indented sub-bullets: '– Premise.json, arc.json, sequence.json – give agent global view of the movie' and '– All scenes generated so far in this sequence and the previous sequence – give agent local context for what should happen next'. The third bullet states: 'Primary scene elements'. This bullet has two indented sub-bullets: '– Slug line – location of the scene' and '– Blocks – series of actions and dialogues in the scene'. The fourth and final bullet reads: 'This agent then creates a file scenes.json'.

The slide is titled 'Scenes.json' in large, bold blue text, centered at the top. Below the title, a code snippet is displayed against a dark background, formatted like a JSON file in a code editor. The visible JSON content shows the beginning of a 'scenes' array. Inside this array, an object represents the first scene, with 'id': 'S0001', 'slug_id': 'SEQ1-01', 'slugline': 'INT. LINK'S HOME - NIGHT', and 'sequence_id': 'SEQ1'. It then shows a 'blocks' array, detailing the scene's action and dialogue. The first three blocks are 'action' describing Link sleeping, hearing a whisper, and waking up. The fourth block is 'dialogue' from 'ZELDA (V.O.)' saying 'Link... help me... I'm trapped in the castle... Aghanim... has me...'. The fifth block is 'action', describing Link clutching his head with visions of Hyrule Castle, Zelda, and a sorcerer. The JSON snippet is truncated at the bottom, indicating more content beyond the visible area.

The slide is titled 'Enhancement Agents' in large, bold blue text, centered at the top. Below the title, on the left, a black, left-aligned bullet point states: 'We can improve the agentic flow by using agents to enhance intermediate outputs'. This bullet has three indented sub-bullets: '– Write better sequences or scenes', '– Clean up the premise', and '– Make the arc more creative'. To the right of the bullet points, a small image of a golden robot labeled 'ENHANCEMENT AGENT' is displayed. Below these elements, a horizontal flowchart diagram, similar to a previous slide, illustrates the 'Screenplay Flow'. It shows a blue rectangular box labeled 'Premise' with 'premise.json', a purple rectangular box labeled 'Arc' with 'arc.json', an orange rectangular box labeled 'Sequences' with 'sequence.json', and a green rectangular box labeled 'Scenes' with 'scenes.json'. Each box has a small robot icon. Additionally, small robot icons are positioned on the arrows pointing between the 'Premise' and 'Arc' boxes, and between the 'Arc' and 'Scenes' boxes, suggesting 'enhancement agents' at these stages of the flow.

The slide is titled 'Generalizing to Other Documents' in large, bold blue text, centered at the top. Below the title, a black, left-aligned bullet point states: 'By modifying the agent prompts, this hierarchical flow can be used for other long text documents'. Below the bullet point, a table is displayed with six columns and six rows. The column headers are: 'Document', 'Premise', 'Arc', 'Sequence', 'Scene', and 'Artist Rendering'.

The first column, 'Document', lists five types of documents: 'Book', 'Podcast', 'Song', 'Lecture', and 'Business plan'.

The 'Book' row maps these stages to: 'Premise', 'Table of contents', 'Chapter', and 'Sub-chapter'. The 'Artist Rendering' column for 'Book' shows a small image of the A.W.E.S.O.M.-O cardboard robot.

The 'Podcast' row maps to: 'Concept', 'Flow, format', 'Segment', and 'Beat'. The 'Artist Rendering' column for 'Podcast' shows a small image of a robot sitting at a desk with headphones.

The 'Song' row maps to: 'Premise', 'Structure (verse, hooks, coda)', 'Verse', and 'Couplet'. The 'Artist Rendering' column for 'Song' shows a small image of a robot on a stage with musical instruments.

The 'Lecture' row maps to: 'Learning objective', 'Outline', 'Module', and 'Slide'. The 'Artist Rendering' column for 'Lecture' shows a small image of a robot giving a presentation at a podium.

The 'Business plan' row maps to: 'Value prop', 'Outline', 'Section', and 'Sub-section'. The 'Artist Rendering' column for 'Business plan' shows a small image of a robot in an office looking at a screen with a business plan.

The slide is titled 'Coding Session' in large, bold blue text, centered at the top. Below the title, there are three black, left-aligned bullet points. The first bullet states: 'Write a short screenplay using Awesome-O'. The second bullet reads: 'Add enhancement agents to the agentic flow'. The third and final bullet states: 'Modify Awesome-O for different document types'. Below the bullet points, a horizontal flowchart diagram, similar to previous 'Screenplay Flow' slides, illustrates the document generation process. It shows a blue rectangular box labeled 'Premise' with 'premise.json', a purple rectangular box labeled 'Arc' with 'arc.json', an orange rectangular box labeled 'Sequences' with 'sequence.json', and a green rectangular box labeled 'Scenes' with 'scenes.json'. Each box has a small robot icon. Additionally, small robot icons are positioned on the arrows pointing between the 'Premise' and 'Arc' boxes, between the 'Arc' and 'Sequences' boxes, and between the 'Arc' and 'Scenes' boxes, representing the enhancement agents at these stages.
